enumeration_source: Wikipedia, 'Names of God in Islam' (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Names_of_God_in_Islam), which presents the 99 in the standard numbered order of the al-Walid ibn Muslim narration reported by al-Tirmidhi (Jami' al-Tirmidhi, Kitab al-Da'awat). NOTE: This enumeration is the specific listing of the 99 that appears in al-Walid ibn Muslim's transmission via al-Tirmidhi. Scholars (e.g. Ibn Taymiyyah, Ibn Hajar, al-Nawawi) note that this particular list of which 99 are intended is understood to be the narrator's (al-Walid's) own compilation/ijtihad inserted (idraj) into the hadith, NOT a definitively prophetic enumeration. The authentic prophetic hadith (Bukhari 2736 / Muslim 2677) affirms that Allah has 99 names and that whoever 'ahsaha' (enumerates/internalises them) enters Paradise, but does not itself list them. This file uses the al-Walid/Tirmidhi listing only as a practical, well-known scaffold for planning coverage. Transliteration and conventional English meanings are taken from the same reference; all Arabic forms were grounded separately via the quran.ai MCP (never written from memory) and are tagged with the ayah fetched. Where a name does not occur in the Qur'an in its definite (al-) name-form, the 'arabic' field holds the grounded Qur'anic derivation actually fetched and a 'note' records the gap.
plan: Build the remaining names in Buruja brand voice, grounded in the Qur'an (quran.ai) plus classical works on the Names (al-Sa'di's Tafsir and his treatise on the Names, Ibn al-Qayyim's Madarij al-Salikin and the an-Nuniyya context, Ibn Kathir). One name per entry, same story-json schema as the current pillar. Names not attested in the Qur'an in definite name-form (see per-entry notes) must be handled carefully: ground them via their Qur'anic root/verbal occurrence and lean on the classical Names literature (al-Sa'di / Ibn al-Qayyim) for the name itself rather than implying a definite Qur'anic name-form that is not there.

grounding.method: Arabic forms fetched via quran.ai MCP (fetch_quran / search_quran, edition ar-simple-clean). Enumeration + transliteration + English from the Wikipedia 'Names of God in Islam' page.
grounding.covered_logic: A canonical name is 'covered' if a current track lesson (lessons.json) teaches it. The intro 'Allah' and the extra-99 attributes (Ar-Rabb, An-Naseer, Al-Qareeb, Dhul-Fadl al-Adheem) are intentionally NOT mapped to the 99. Note: the list contains 'Al-Wali' twice - #55 (waliyy, the Protecting Friend/Ally) and #77 (wali, the Patron/Governor); the lesson al-wali-an-nasir teaches #55, so #77 is treated as remaining.

names.en: The Giver of Security / The Affirmer of Faith
The Almighty / The Exalted in Might

names.note: Grounded form الْغَفَّار is in the track lesson al-ghaffar; primary_ayah 20:82 (Ta-Ha) carries la-ghaffar; 59:23-24 batch confirms sibling names.
Taught in al-wahhab-al-fattah; primary_ayah 38:9 (al-Wahhab); the 99-batch fetches did not re-fetch this ayah but the name is in the covered lesson's grounded set.
Taught in al-wahhab-al-fattah; al-Fattah grounded at 34:26 (al-fattah al-alim).
Taught in al-alim; al-Alim grounded across many ayat (e.g. 2:32, 6:115 'as-sami al-alim').

Not in Qur'an in definite name-form الْقَابِض; from root ق-ب-ض. Grounded verbal form يَقْبِضُ ('He withholds') at 2:245 (wa-Llahu yaqbidu wa-yabsut). The name is established in the al-Walid/Tirmidhi list and classical Names literature, not as a Qur'anic definite name.

names.en: The Extender / The Expander of provision
names.note: Not in Qur'an in definite name-form الْبَاسِط; from root ب-س-ط. Grounded verbal form يَبْسُطُ ('He extends/expands') at 2:245; cf. yabsutu al-rizq 29:62.

names.en: The Abaser / The One who brings low
names.note: Not in Qur'an in definite name-form الْخَافِض; from root خ-ف-ض. Grounded as خَافِضَة ('bringing [some] low') at 56:3, describing the Hour. Often paired with Ar-Rafi in the Names tradition.

names.en: The Exalter / The One who raises
names.note: Not in Qur'an in definite name-form الرَّافِع; from root ر-ف-ع. Grounded as رَافِعَة ('raising [others] up') at 56:3; cf. رَفِيعُ الدَّرَجَاتِ (Rafi' al-darajat) 40:15.
Not in Qur'an in definite name-form الْمُعِزّ; from root ع-ز-ز (form IV). Grounded as تُعِزُّ مَن تَشَاءُ ('You honour whom You will') at 3:26.

names.en: The Giver of Dishonour / The Humiliator
names.note: Not in Qur'an in definite name-form الْمُذِلّ; from root ذ-ل-ل (form IV). Grounded as تُذِلُّ مَن تَشَاءُ ('You abase whom You will') at 3:26.

Not in Qur'an in definite name-form الْحَكَم; from root ح-ك-م. Grounded via لَهُ الْحُكْمُ ('His is the judgement') at 28:88 and بِأَحْكَمِ الْحَاكِمِينَ 95:8. The name Al-Hakam is in the Sunnah (kunya Abu'l-Hakam context) and the Names list.
Not in Qur'an in definite name-form الْعَدْل; from root ع-د-ل. Grounded as وَعَدْلًا ('and in justice') at 6:115 (tammat kalimatu rabbika sidqan wa-adlan).
Taught in al-latif-al-khabir; al-Latif grounded at 42:19 (Allahu latifun bi-ibadih ... al-qawiyy al-aziz).
Taught in al-latif-al-khabir; al-Khabir grounded at 6:18 (al-hakim al-khabir).
Taught in ash-shakur-al-halim; al-Halim grounded e.g. 3:155 (ghafurun halim), 2:225.
Taught in al-azim; al-Azim grounded at 2:255 (Ayat al-Kursi: wa-huwa al-aliyy al-azim).
Taught alongside al-Ghaffar in al-ghaffar; al-Ghafur grounded at 10:107 (al-ghafur al-rahim).
Taught in ash-shakur-al-halim; ash-Shakur grounded e.g. 35:30, 35:34, 64:17.
Taught in al-ali-al-mutaali; al-Ali grounded at 2:255 and 22:62 (al-aliyy al-kabir).
Grounded at 22:62 and 13:9 (al-kabir al-mutaal). Distinct from Al-Ali; not taught as its own lesson.
Not in Qur'an in definite name-form الْحَفِيظ; from root ح-ف-ظ. Grounded as حَفِيظ ('a Guardian/Preserver') at 11:57 (inna rabbi ala kulli shay'in hafiz); cf. 34:21, 42:6.
Not in Qur'an in definite name-form الْمُقِيت; appears once as مُقِيتًا (indefinite/accusative) at 4:85 (wa-kana Allahu ala kulli shay'in muqita).

names.en: The Reckoner / The Bringer of Account
names.note: Not in Qur'an in definite name-form الْحَسِيب; appears as حَسِيبًا / حَسِيب (indefinite) at 4:86, 4:6, 33:39 (kafa bi-Llahi hasiba).
Not in Qur'an in definite name-form الْجَلِيل; from root ج-ل-ل. Grounded via ذُو الْجَلَالِ وَالْإِكْرَامِ at 55:27 and ذِي الْجَلَالِ 55:78. (Dhul-Jalal wal-Ikram itself is name #85.)
Taught in al-karim; al-Karim grounded e.g. 82:6 (rabbika al-karim), 27:40 (ghaniyyun karim).
Taught in ar-raqib; Raqib grounded e.g. 5:117 (anta al-raqib), 4:1, 33:52 (raqiba).
Taught in al-qarib-al-mujib; Mujib grounded at 11:61 (qaribun mujib).
Taught in al-wasi; al-Wasi grounded at 2:268 (wa-Llahu wasiun alim), 2:261.
Taught in al-aziz-al-hakim; al-Hakim grounded at 59:24 (al-aziz al-hakim).
Taught in al-wadud; al-Wadud grounded at 85:14 (al-ghafur al-wadud), 11:90.
Grounded as الْمَجِيد at 85:15 (Dhu'l-Arsh al-Majid); cf. majidun 11:73. Distinct from #65 Al-Majid (al-Wajid/al-Majid pairing); both share the form الْمَجِيد.

names.en: The Resurrector / The Raiser of the dead
names.note: Not in Qur'an in definite name-form الْبَاعِث; from root ب-ع-ث. Grounded as يَبْعَثُ ('He will resurrect') at 22:7 (anna Allaha yab'athu man fi'l-qubur).
Grounded as شَهِيد at 22:17 (inna Allaha ala kulli shay'in shahid); usually appears indefinite/predicative rather than definite الشهيد, but is a well-attested Qur'anic descriptor.
Taught in al-haqq; al-Haqq grounded at 22:62 (anna Allaha huwa al-haqq), 20:114.

names.en: The Trustee / The Disposer of Affairs
names.note: Usually appears indefinite/predicative وَكِيل / وَكِيلًا (e.g. 4:81 'kafa bi-Llahi wakila', 73:9), not definite الوكيل; well-attested Qur'anic descriptor.
Taught in al-qawiyy-al-matin; al-Qawi grounded at 42:19 (al-qawiyy al-aziz), 11:66.
Taught in al-qawiyy-al-matin; al-Matin grounded at 51:58 (dhu'l-quwwati al-matin).
waliyy-form. Taught in al-wali-an-nasir (as Al-Wali, the Protecting Guardian/close Ally); grounded e.g. 42:28 (huwa al-waliyy al-hamid), 2:257. Distinct from #77 Al-Wali (wali, the Patron/Governor).
Taught alongside al-Ghani in al-ghani; al-Hamid grounded at 22:64 (al-ghaniyy al-hamid), 11:73 (hamidun majid).

names.en: The All-Enumerator / The Reckoner of all
names.note: Not in Qur'an in definite name-form الْمُحْصِي; from root ح-ص-ي. Grounded as أَحْصَاهُمْ ('He has enumerated them') at 19:94; cf. ahsa kulla shay'in 72:28.

names.en: The Originator / The Initiator of creation
names.note: Not in Qur'an in definite name-form الْمُبْدِئ; from root ب-د-أ. Grounded as يُبْدِئُ ('He originates') at 85:13 (innahu huwa yubdi'u wa-yu'id); cf. yabda'u al-khalq 30:27.
Not in Qur'an in definite name-form الْمُعِيد; from root ع-و-د. Grounded as يُعِيدُ ('He repeats/restores') at 85:13; cf. thumma yu'iduhu 30:27.
Not in Qur'an as the standalone definite name الْمُحْيِي; from root ح-ي-ي. Grounded as participle لَمُحْيِي الْمَوْتَىٰ ('the Giver of life to the dead') at 30:50; cf. verb yuhyi 41:39, 50:43.
Not in Qur'an in definite name-form الْمُمِيت; from root م-و-ت. Grounded as نُمِيتُ ('We cause death') at 50:43 (inna nahnu nuhyi wa-numit); cf. yumitu 40:68.
Taught in al-hayy-al-qayyum; al-Hayy grounded at 2:255 (al-hayy al-qayyum), 3:2, 40:65.
Taught in al-hayy-al-qayyum; al-Qayyum grounded at 2:255 (Ayat al-Kursi), 3:2, 20:111.
Not in Qur'an in definite name-form الْوَاجِد; from root و-ج-د. Grounded as وَجَدْنَاهُ ('We found him') at 38:44. The name is from the al-Walid/Tirmidhi list and classical Names literature.

Second 'Al-Majid' in the list (the first is #48). Both grounded by الْمَجِيد at 85:15 (Dhu'l-Arsh al-Majid) and مَجِيد at 11:73. The tradition treats them as a duplicate/near-duplicate; build as one teaching unless the brand wants the al-Wajid/al-Majid pairing distinguished.
Taught in al-awwal-al-akhir; al-Wahid grounded at 13:16 (al-wahid al-qahhar), 39:4.
Taught in al-awwal-al-akhir; al-Ahad grounded at 112:1 (qul huwa Allahu ahad).

names.en: The Eternal Refuge / The Self-Sufficient Master
names.note: Taught alongside al-Ghani in al-ghani; as-Samad grounded at 112:2 (Allahu as-samad).
Taught in al-qadir; Qadir grounded widely (e.g. 'ala kulli shay'in qadir' 2:20, 5:120). Definite الْقَادِر appears e.g. 6:65.
Grounded as مُّقْتَدِر at 54:55 (malikin muqtadir) and 54:42; definite الْمُقْتَدِر at 18:45. Build from these.

names.en: The Expediter / The One who brings forward
names.note: Not in Qur'an in definite name-form الْمُقَدِّم; from root ق-د-م (form II). Grounded via قَدَّمْتُ ('I put forth') at 50:28; the Muqaddim/Mu'akhkhir pairing rests on the Sunnah and Names literature.

names.en: The Delayer / The One who puts back
names.note: Not in Qur'an in definite name-form الْمُؤَخِّر; from root ء-خ-ر (form II). Grounded as يُؤَخِّرُهُمْ ('He defers them') at 14:42, yu'akhkhirkum 71:4.
Taught in al-awwal-al-akhir; al-Awwal grounded at 57:3 (huwa al-awwal wa'l-akhir).
Taught in al-awwal-al-akhir; al-Akhir grounded at 57:3.

wali-form (distinct from #55 waliyy). Grounded as وَالٍ ('any patron/protector besides Him') at 13:11 (wa-ma lahum min dunihi min wal). Treated as remaining because the covered lesson al-wali-an-nasir teaches the waliyy-form (#55).
Taught in al-ali-al-mutaali; al-Mutaali grounded at 13:9 (al-kabir al-mutaal).

names.en: The Most Kind / The Source of Goodness

names.note: Not in Qur'an in definite name-form الْمُنْتَقِم; from root ن-ق-م. Grounded as مُنتَقِمُونَ ('We are avengers') at 32:22; cf. dhu'ntiqam 3:4. Usually expressed via the phrase 'Dhu'ntiqam' in the Names tradition.

names.en: The Pardoner / The Effacer of sins
names.note: Usually appears indefinite/predicative عَفُوّ / عَفُوًّا (e.g. 4:43 'afuwwan ghafura', 4:99, 22:60), not as standalone definite العفو; well-attested Qur'anic descriptor.
Usually appears indefinite/predicative رَءُوف (e.g. 9:117 'ra'ufun rahim', 2:143, 24:20), not as standalone definite الرؤوف; well-attested Qur'anic descriptor.
Taught alongside al-Malik in al-malik (Malik al-Mulk, Master of all dominion); grounded at 3:26 (qul Allahumma malika'l-mulk).
Taught alongside al-Karim in al-karim; grounded at 55:27 (dhu'l-jalal wa'l-ikram), 55:78.

names.en: The Equitable / The Dispenser of Justice
names.note: Not in Qur'an in definite name-form الْمُقْسِط; from root ق-س-ط. Grounded via بِالْقِسْطِ ('with equity') at 7:29; Allah loves al-muqsitin (الْمُقْسِطِين) 5:42, 49:9, 60:8.
Not in Qur'an in standalone definite name-form الْجَامِع; grounded as جَامِعُ النَّاسِ ('Gatherer of mankind') in construct at 3:9 (innaka jami'u'n-nas).
Taught in al-ghani; al-Ghani grounded at 22:64 (al-ghaniyy al-hamid), 35:15.
Not in Qur'an in definite name-form الْمُغْنِي; from root غ-ن-ي (form IV). Grounded as يُغْنِيكُمُ اللَّهُ ('Allah will enrich you') at 9:28; cf. aghna 53:48 (huwa aghna wa-aqna).

Not in Qur'an in definite name-form الْمَانِع; root م-ن-ع does not occur as a divine name. The withholding sense is grounded via أَمْسَكَ رِزْقَهُ ('[if] He withheld His provision') at 67:21 and 'ma yumsik' at 35:2. The name rests on the Sunnah and Names literature; handle carefully.

names.en: The Distresser / The Creator of harm (with wisdom)
names.note: Not in Qur'an in definite name-form الضَّارّ; from root ض-ر-ر. Grounded via بِضُرّ ('with harm') at 6:17 (in yamsaska Allahu bi-durr). Usually paired with An-Nafi; both rest on the Names tradition. Handle carefully.

names.en: The Benefiter / The Bringer of benefit
names.note: Not in Qur'an in definite name-form النَّافِع; from root ن-ف-ع. Grounded via لَا يَنفَعُهُمْ ('what neither harms nor benefits them' - of idols) at 10:18; the benefit is affirmed of Allah throughout. Paired with Ad-Darr in the tradition. Handle carefully.

Grounded as اللَّهُ نُورُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ('Allah is the Light of the heavens and the earth') at 24:35; appears in construct (Light of...) rather than the bare definite النور, but is the direct Qur'anic basis for the name.
Taught in al-hadi; grounded via 'inna Allaha la-hadi alladhina amanu' 22:54; cf. 'kafa bi-rabbika hadiyan wa-nasira' 25:31. Definite الهادي not standalone in Qur'an.
Not in Qur'an in standalone definite name-form الْبَدِيع; grounded as بَدِيعُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ('Originator of the heavens and the earth') in construct at 2:117, 6:101.
Not in Qur'an in definite name-form الْبَاقِي; from root ب-ق-ي. Grounded as وَيَبْقَىٰ وَجْهُ رَبِّكَ ('and the Face of your Lord will remain') at 55:27; cf. 'wa-ma inda Allahi baq' 16:96.

names.en: The Inheritor / The Heir of all
names.note: Not in Qur'an in standalone definite name-form الْوَارِث; from root و-ر-ث. Grounded as نَرِثُ الْأَرْضَ ('We will inherit the earth') at 19:40; cf. khayru'l-warithin 21:89, nahnu al-warithun 15:23.

names.en: The Guide to the Right Path / The Infallible Teacher
names.note: Not in Qur'an as a name OF ALLAH in definite form. The word الرَّشِيد occurs at 11:87 but there it is spoken by Shu'ayb's people TO Shu'ayb ('you are the forbearing, the rightly-guided') - i.e. of a human, used ironically. The name for Allah rests on the al-Walid/Tirmidhi list and Names literature (rushd, ر-ش-د); ground via the root, not via 11:87 as a divine attribute. Handle carefully.

Not in Qur'an in any name-form الصَّبُور; root ص-ب-ر occurs abundantly as command/verb but never as a divine name in the Qur'an. Nearest grounded participle is صَابِرًا (of Prophet Ayyub) at 38:44. The name As-Sabur rests entirely on the al-Walid/Tirmidhi list and the Names literature; handle carefully.
